resources.string: The velvet hides barbs; every touch forgets where the self ends.
VELVET HIDES BARBS. EVERY TOUCH ERASES THE SELF.

Silk drinks sweat and blood until the two shine the same. Familiar whispers press close to the ear, soft enough to be trusted, sharp enough to cut. Touch arrives like voltage. Pain and pleasure trade names and then lose them. At the summit of delight, the soul is not held; it is shattered into sensation, and every fragment thanks the abyss for its embrace.
SILK DRINKS SWEAT AND BLOOD. WHISPERS CUT. TOUCH STRIKES LIKE VOLTAGE. PAIN AND PLEASURE LOSE THEIR NAMES. THE SELF SHATTERS.
